title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 1, 2026
headline: India crosses from Developing into Critical (22.7 → 15.6, delta −7.1) as HRW World Report 2026 and the Polis Project document a government-directed refoulement-and-expulsion regime: 1,500+ expelled to Bangladesh without due process, 192 UNHCR Rohingya refouled, 40 Rohingya beaten and thrown from a navy vessel into the sea, and 10,000+ Gujarat demolitions defying the Supreme Court — the second application of the ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT ruling in two cycles.
summary: A single scored movement defines tonight's cycle: India's composite falls from 22.7 to 15.6 (delta −7.1), crossing from Developing into Critical — the benchmark's first Critical-band placement for a large-democracy index member. The ruling is the second application of ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT (established last cycle for Burkina Faso), extended tonight with a new refinement: where functioning democratic courts demonstrably contest and reverse some acts of a state-perpetrated regime, the score is held above the absolute floor and above the multi-sided active-perpetration case. India at 15.6 sits above Burkina Faso (6.3, multi-sided conflict with residual UN cooperation) and the uncontested sole-perpetrator floor (Myanmar, Sudan, South Sudan at 0.0), documenting a three-tier internal structure within the Critical band. Three new methodology rulings are established: CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR (South Sudan IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe at 73,300, +160%, is conflict-driven siege-and-starvation conduct sustaining the 0.0 floor — distinguished from the natural-disaster overlay); ANNOUNCED-NOT-VERIFIED-IMPROVEMENT (UnitedHealth prior-authorization reductions are announced but not verified for delivered effect and are not creditable against the near-floor 10.2 baseline); and OCCUPATION-ATTRIBUTION-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT (Gaza catastrophic conditions attributed to the occupying power and scored against Israel at 0.0, not the Palestinian Authority at 25.0). The Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC is accelerating: DRC reached 321 cases and 48 deaths while Uganda confirmed 15 cases including Kampala transmission — both entities confirm under the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY conditional on the absence of deliberate state obstruction. Oracle remains 0.6 points above Critical with the WARN adjudication window closing June 15. Hungary confirms at 50.2 as the June 1 constitutional-amendment launch materializes the boundary-watch resolution from 2026-05-30.

topSignals.title: India 22.7 → 15.6 (Delta −7.1): BAND CROSSING Developing → Critical — Government-Directed Refoulement-and-Expulsion Regime
topSignals.description: HRW World Report 2026 and the Polis Project document a government-directed regime of refoulement and unlawful expulsion. Bangladesh confirmed 1,500+ men, women, and children expelled May–June without due process, including Indian citizens — a pregnant woman (Sunali Khatun) was deported and the Calcutta High Court issued a return order. 192 UNHCR-registered Rohingya refugees were unlawfully expelled. 40 Rohingya were placed on a navy vessel, beaten, given life jackets, and thrown into the sea near Myanmar; the UN Special Rapporteur documented blatant disregard for the lives and safety of protected persons. The May 2 SOP eliminated the bilateral-notification requirement before deportation; the Union Home Ministry ordered all states to set up 30-day Special Task Forces; the Immigration and Foreigners Bill 2025 mandates detention centres in every state. 10,000+ Gujarat demolitions proceeded in direct defiance of the Supreme Court's November 2024 ruling. 64 documented anti-Muslim hate-speech incidents followed the April Pahalgam attack. The ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT ruling (established 2026-05-31, Burkina Faso) is extended with a new refinement: India is scored to 15.6, not the absolute floor, because functioning courts (Calcutta HC return order) demonstrably contest and reverse some acts. Downgrade concentrated in EQU (1.8→1.3), EMP (1.9→1.4), ACC (1.9→1.4), INT (2.0→1.5). This is the first Critical-band placement for a large-democracy benchmark member and establishes the third tier in the Critical band's internal structure.

evidence.quote: Bangladesh authorities reported that India expelled more than 1,500 men, women, and children in May and June. However, several of them turned out to be Indian citizens, mostly impoverished migrant workers from states bordering Bangladesh.
evidence.claim: India expelled more than 1,500 people to Bangladesh without due process, including its own citizens.
evidence.source: Human Rights Watch, World Report 2026 (India chapter)

evidence.quote: In May, authorities put 40 Rohingya refugees on a navy ship. The crew allegedly beat and interrogated the refugees, gave them life jackets, and then tossed them into the sea near the Myanmar coast.
evidence.claim: India unlawfully expelled UNHCR-registered Rohingya refugees, including 40 placed on a navy ship and put into the sea near Myanmar.
evidence.source: Human Rights Watch, World Report 2026 (India chapter)

evidence.quote: authorities in Gujarat state demolished over 10,000 structures claiming they were occupied by 'illegal Bangladeshi immigrants,' going against the Supreme Court's November 2024 ruling that outlawed such arbitrary and punitive demolitions.
evidence.claim: Gujarat authorities demolished over 10,000 structures in direct defiance of the Supreme Court's November 2024 ruling against arbitrary demolitions.
evidence.source: Human Rights Watch, World Report 2026 (India chapter)

topSignals.title: South Sudan 0.0 — CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR Ruling Distinguishes Active-Conflict Siege from Natural-Disaster Overlay
topSignals.description: South Sudan's IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe surged to 73,300 people (+160% from prior period), with 7.8M (56% of the population) in Phase 3 or above. HRW's May 6 documentation confirms civilians are starving in conflict areas where SAF and SPLM-IO fighting cuts communities off from food and markets, with constrained humanitarian access. This is not a natural-disaster or incapacity case: the famine is substantially conflict-driven, with active belligerents controlling access. The new CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR ruling distinguishes this from the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT: where IPC Phase 5 escalation is substantially driven by active conflict with state-attributable constrained humanitarian access, the conduct sustains the active-perpetration 0.0 floor. An entity already at the absolute floor cannot move lower; the escalation reinforces the floor designation. This ruling will govern future conflict-famine disambiguation in Sudan, DRC, and Afghanistan.

topSignals.title: UnitedHealth Group 10.2 Confirmed — ANNOUNCED-NOT-VERIFIED-IMPROVEMENT Ruling Holds Prior-Auth Reductions Non-Creditable
topSignals.description: UnitedHealthcare's prior-authorization reductions — 30% reduction in general denials, approximately two-thirds reduction in pediatric denials, and a 50-insurer June 2026 pledge — are a genuine ACT/EQU-relevant access-improvement signal. The new ANNOUNCED-NOT-VERIFIED-IMPROVEMENT ruling holds: these reductions are announced and in-progress, not yet verified for delivered protective effect. Credit requires demonstrated protective effect — symmetric with the standard applied to Haiti's incoming peacekeeping capacity last cycle. The negatives remain priced: DOJ criminal and civil MA-billing probe (disclosed July 2025), AI claim-denial class action in discovery, and the Senate's documented finding of aggressive MA gaming. Net-neutral against a near-floor baseline. The prior-auth improvement signal is logged as a forward watch; verified delivery of the reductions would constitute an ACT/EQU upgrade trigger.

topSignals.title: Oracle 20.6 — WARN Final-Phase Sign-or-Forfeit Active Through June 15; Boundary Sensitivity Unchanged
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act last-working-day window is in its final phase (June 1 sign-or-forfeit deadline, completing June 15, approximately 30,000 affected workers). Oracle declined to renegotiate severance terms, consistent with the priced EMP 1.2 score. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory and the New Jersey state WARN matter remain filed-but-unadjudicated under the established FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED-LITIGATION ruling — not yet independently scorable. The entity sits 0.6 points above the Critical boundary; any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the WARN-avoidance theory before or after the June 15 window closes constitutes a scoring trigger from a boundary-sensitive baseline.

topSignals.title: Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC Reaches Kampala — DRC 321 Cases, Uganda 15 Cases with Capital Transmission Confirmed
topSignals.description: DRC's Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak escalated to 321 cases and 48 deaths (up from 282 and 42 last cycle). Uganda has confirmed 15 cases including a death with documented Kampala transmission — the outbreak has reached the Ugandan capital for the first time. WHO declared a PHEIC on May 17. Uganda's documented state posture is active cooperation: surveillance systems operational, isolation facilities deployed, National Emergency Medical Teams engaged, mobile laboratory in the field, WHO/CDC coordination confirmed. Under the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT ruling, both DRC (2.3) and Uganda (20.3) confirm on the basis of priced incapacity rather than state perpetration — the overlay hold is conditional on the absence of deliberate obstruction. The June–July peak window is active with no approved vaccine and constrained international response funding.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Any new independently dated mass-expulsion event with verified due-process violations, Supreme Court contempt enforcement on Gujarat demolitions, or a halt/reversal of the STF deportation directive would each constitute distinct scoring triggers from the 15.6 baseline.
boundaryWatch.note: India's proposed score of 15.6 places it in the third tier of the Critical band's internal structure — above Burkina Faso (6.3, multi-sided conflict) and the uncontested sole-perpetrator floor (0.0). The nationwide STF deportation scale-up is ongoing; the functioning-courts mitigant is the load-bearing distinction from the lower tiers.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day window closes June 15; any adjudicated finding on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in NJ state proceedings or the Washington class action constitutes a scoring trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 20.6 is 0.6pt above the Critical boundary — the narrowest active margin in the Fortune 500 cluster. The WARN window enters its final two weeks; the reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is pre-positioned for promotion to a scored category upon adjudication.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth — a favorable ruling would credit the DoD-terms refusal as an I1 governance signal and trigger the Functional/Established crossing; an adverse ruling produces downward movement from 59.1.
boundaryWatch.note: Cycle 13. The DC Circuit oral arguments were divided as of May 2026 with no ruling issued. Anthropic's RSP v3.0 pre-commitment pledge reduction is a documented INT negative already priced at 3.2; the DoD refusal is the outstanding positive signal awaiting judicial resolution.

emergingRisks.risk: India's nationwide STF deportation regime is ongoing; the May 2 SOP eliminating bilateral notification and the Union Home Ministry 30-day directive are active standing instruments of the expulsion conduct documented in tonight's proposal.
emergingRisks.detail: The proposed 15.6 baseline is calibrated on the current conduct pattern documented in HRW World Report 2026 and the Polis Project. The functioning-courts mitigant (Calcutta HC return orders) is the distinguishing variable from the absolute floor. Any documented continuity of the sea-endangerment conduct, a Supreme Court contempt action on Gujarat demolitions, or a large-scale new deportation event that extends beyond the window already priced would each constitute independent scoring triggers downward. Conversely, a formal halt of the deportation regime and documented return of expelled persons would constitute an upgrade trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle sits 0.6 points above the Critical boundary with the WARN adjudication window closing June 15 — the narrowest active margin in the Fortune 500 cluster entering a known deadline.
emergingRisks.detail: The ALGORITHM-BASED-WORKER-CLASSIFICATION candidate (remote-worker reclassification as a WARN-avoidance device) is pre-positioned for promotion to a scored category upon adjudication. New Jersey state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action are the two active channels. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding in either channel before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring event from a boundary-sensitive baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC is entering the June–July peak window with confirmed Kampala transmission, no approved vaccine, and constrained international response funding.
emergingRisks.detail: DRC at 321 cases/48 deaths and Uganda at 15 cases including a capital-city death. The NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold for both entities is conditional on documented state cooperation continuing without deliberate obstruction. Uganda's cooperation is confirmed this cycle; DRC's is not documented as obstructed. The outbreak peak window coincides with reduced international health-response capacity post-USAID cuts. Deliberate state obstruction, a failure of isolation protocols in Kampala, or spread to additional countries would each break the overlay hold.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan and South Sudan lean seasons are now active simultaneously — both at the absolute floor (0.0) with ongoing conflict-driven famine in Al Fasher, Kadugli, and Jonglei/Unity states.
emergingRisks.detail: Sudan: 19.5M crisis food insecure, 135,000 IPC Phase 5, plan 20% funded, lean season June–August. South Sudan: IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe 73,300 (+160%); constrained access by SAF/SPLM-IO. Both are already at 0.0; the floor cannot move lower, but the scale of the evidence reinforces the CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR ruling and its distinction from the natural-disaster overlay. The humanitarian response funding gap in both countries deepens through August.

emergingRisks.risk: West and Central Africa lean season covers 52.8M food insecure with Borno, Nigeria at IPC Catastrophe level for the first time in approximately a decade — the natural-disaster-overlay holds absent state obstruction, but the signal load is accumulating.
emergingRisks.detail: Nigeria confirms at 21.9 under the natural-disaster-overlay; Borno 15,000 at Catastrophe. Any documented deliberate state obstruction of lean-season aid access in Borno constitutes a scoring trigger toward the Critical boundary (currently 1.9pt away). Burkina Faso (6.3) sits in the same geographic cluster and is not in tonight's rotation; the lean season amplifies the already-established active-perpetration score.

emergingRisks.risk: Hungary's constitutional-amendment process is underway with Venice Commission consultation active and Sulyok's refusal to resign on the record.
emergingRisks.detail: The formal amendment launch on June 1 is the materialization of the boundary-watch resolved last cycle. The amendment is expected to complete approximately one month from June 1. Sulyok removal or a Venice Commission adverse opinion are the two outcome branches. Hungary is now at 50.2 (INT-cluster proposal applied 2026-06-01); a successful Sulyok removal via the amendment would constitute a further INT/BND downward signal. The EU RRF August 31 milestone adds a second accountability track.

emergingRisks.risk: OpenAI's comprehensive assessment is overdue — the DoW classified-network agreement, PBC conversion finalization, and EU AI Act August 2 transparency deadline are all unpriced in the published 27.5.
emergingRisks.detail: OpenAI was last assessed on a narrow cycle basis confirming 27.5. The DoW agreement constitutes a scorable event that has not received a full assessment. The PBC conversion finalization terms and the EU AI Act August 2 GPAI transparency obligation are both known forward signals. This is the highest-priority unscheduled full assessment in the AI labs index.

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT
methodologyNotes.description: Extended tonight with the 'functioning-institutions-hold-above-floor' refinement. Where a corroborated Tier-5 finding documents government-directed atrocity or systematic rights-violation conduct not reflected in the published baseline, reweight toward — but not necessarily to — the Critical floor. Score to the absolute 0.0 floor only when (a) no other non-state actor perpetrates independently at comparable scale and (b) state cooperation with international humanitarian actors is absent. New refinement: where functioning democratic institutions (courts, opposition legislatures, independent prosecutors) demonstrably contest and reverse some acts of the state-perpetrated regime, the score is held above both the uncontested sole-perpetrator floor and the multi-sided-conflict case. India (15.6) is the anchor case for the functioning-institutions refinement; Burkina Faso (6.3, 2026-05-31) is the anchor case for multi-sided conflict with residual cooperation; Myanmar and Sudan (0.0) are the anchor cases for uncontested perpetration.

methodologyNotes.name: CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established tonight for South Sudan. Where IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe escalation is substantially driven by active conflict cutting communities off from food and markets — with state-attributed or belligerent-attributed constrained humanitarian access — the conduct sustains the active-perpetration 0.0 floor rather than triggering the natural-disaster overlay. Distinct from NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT (exogenous shock revealing priced incapacity, government cooperating). An entity already at the absolute floor cannot move lower; the escalation reinforces the floor designation. South Sudan (0.0, SAF/SPLM-IO) is the anchor case; Sudan (0.0, RSF siege) is the co-anchor.

methodologyNotes.name: ANNOUNCED-NOT-VERIFIED-IMPROVEMENT
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established tonight for UnitedHealth Group. Where a corporate entity announces or initiates a policy change with genuine protective potential (access improvements, harm-reduction programs, structural changes) but the delivered protective effect has not been independently verified, the improvement is not yet creditable as an upgrade trigger. The standard is symmetric with the Haiti incoming-peacekeeping-capacity standard applied in prior cycles: announced or in-progress capacity is not scored until delivery is documented. On a near-floor entity with structural conduct deficits, announced improvements do not offset priced negatives. Credit requires verified delivered effect; reassess on documented outcomes. UnitedHealth prior-auth reductions (30% general; ~two-thirds pediatric) are the anchor case.

methodologyNotes.name: OCCUPATION-ATTRIBUTION-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established tonight for Palestine/Israel. Where catastrophic humanitarian conditions in an occupied territory are substantially attributable to the occupying power's conduct (aid restrictions, access denial, bombardment) and those conditions are already priced into the occupying power's score, they are not additionally scored against a separate entity representing the occupied population's governing authority. Gaza conditions (77% acute food insecurity, 84% economic contraction, reconstruction less than $1B of $71.4B assessed need) are attributable to Israel's AUTHORIZED-RESUMPTION-WITH-SYSTEMATIC-DENIAL conduct (scored at 0.0 floor) and are not independently scored against the Palestinian Authority (25.0). PA-attributable governance acts are assessed separately. Palestine 25.0 is the anchor case.

methodologyNotes.name: FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED-LITIGATION
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed and applied symmetrically across Oracle, CVS Health, and UnitedHealth Group this cycle. Newly-filed or disclosed but pre-adjudication civil and criminal matters — including institutional-plaintiff civil suits, DOJ probes, and class actions in discovery — reinforce already near-floor profiles and are not independently scorable as downgrade triggers. The scoring trigger is adjudication, settlement, or a judicial finding. Consistently applied: Oracle remote-reclassification theory, CVS $250M 340B diversion suit, UnitedHealth DOJ MA-billing probe, AI claim-denial class action.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed for Uganda (Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC reaching Kampala), DRC (321 cases/48 deaths), and Nigeria (West Africa lean season). An exogenous shock reveals already-priced state-response incapacity rather than introducing a new scorable state act. No further downgrade on a near-floor or Developing-band baseline absent documented deliberate state obstruction. Uganda's documented cooperation (surveillance, isolation facilities, National EMT, mobile lab, WHO/CDC coordination) confirms the incapacity-not-perpetration classification. The hold is conditional — deliberate obstruction breaks it.

methodologyNotes.name: MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed not operationalized for Anthropic. Anthropic's refusal of DoD 'all lawful purpose' terms is a consistency-under-pressure signal that is in-progress and not yet creditable pending the DC Circuit ruling. RSP v3.0 dropping the flagship pre-commitment pledge is a candidate negative already priced within INT 3.2. Net-neutral this cycle. Anthropic's holdout from the DoW classified-network access framework is a standing distinction from peer AI labs under the candidate category, not yet a scored dimension signal.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If India's Calcutta High Court return orders are the load-bearing variable holding the score above 6.3, what volume or pattern of documented Supreme Court defiance — as in the Gujarat demolitions — would cause the functioning-courts mitigant to no longer hold under the ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT ruling?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: India scores 15.6 rather than 6.3 or 0.0 on the functioning-courts distinction — but the ruling does not yet specify how much judicial defiance dissolves the mitigant.
